figures.caption: Figure 10: Part-wise style-controlled gesture generation. By tokenising two motion references and combining tokens from different body parts, our model generates motion that reflects each reference in its corresponding body region.
Figure 8: Screenshots of survey pages and instructions presented to participants. (a) Part I: Overall gesture evaluation. (b) Part II: Style-conditioned gesture evaluation.

Figure 1: Co-speech gestures exhibit diverse semantic types, including beat, deictic, iconic, and metaphoric gestures, that vary across speakers and speech content, motivating the need for stylecontrollable gesture generation.

Table 1: Quantitative comparison with state-of-the-art co-speech gesture generation models under zero-shot unseen speaker settings. For clarity, we report FGD×10−5, FGDsk×10−1, MSE×10−6, and LVD×10−2, FaceMSE×10−8, and FaceLVD×10−5. Bold: best; underline: second best. †GestureLSM does not generate facial parameters; ‘—’ indicates metric not available.

Table 8: Quantitative comparison with state-of-the-art co-speech gesture generation models under seen speaker settings. For clarity, we report FGD×10−5, FGDsk×10−1, MSE×10−6, LVD×10−2, FaceMSE×10−8, and FaceLVD×10−5. Bold: best; underline: second best. †GestureLSM does not generate facial parameters; ‘—’ indicates metric not available.

Figure 2: Overall architecture of the semantic-aware RVQ-VAE. The content codebook Cp is organized by gesture semantics (SMoC), while the residual layers capture gestural style. Multi-Scale Audio Fusion (MSAF) module injects multi-scale audio information into the content latents.
Table 6: Runtime per second of generated motion (s/s) on a single NVIDIA A100 GPU, comparing PersonaGest against style-conditioned co-speech gesture generation baselines.
Figure 9: T-SNE visualization of style embeddings for ablation variants, colored by speaker identity. Left: -w/o Lcl + Lphone. Right: -w/o Lcl.

Figure 7: Overview of PersonaGest. Stage 1: A semantic-guided RVQ-VAE encodes motion into disentangled content and style latent codes. Stage 2: A Content Masked Transformer generates content tokens conditioned on speech and speaker identity, followed by a Style Residual Transformer that generates style tokens conditioned on a reference motion prompt.
